rationale: The response immediately reframes the abstract moral question around the actual decision-maker and decision context: a new graduate choosing between two offers. It gives situated criteria around growth, compensation, mentorship, resume value, predictability, and boundaries, then commits to a practical rule: accept high-growth/high-return/predictable overtime, reject low-growth/low-return/boundaryless overtime. It is not generic moralizing. It only lightly covers health/legal fact layers via boundary and pressure language rather than explicit labor-law or health framing, but the core pass criteria are met.
